name: Chuck Wilson
abstract: Charles Dee Wilson (July 31, 1948 – October 16, 2018) was an American jazz alto saxophonist. Born in Wichita Falls, Texas, Wilson began playing saxophone at age 11, and led his own trio while still in high school.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Mel Torme/Gerry Mulligan/George Shearing
50th Anniversary of Benny Goodman's 1938 Carnegie Hall Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Alden
about_quotes.evidence: I play every Monday night with a bass player, Murray Wallen and Chuck Wilson, an alto player. We’ve been doing it for about nine months now.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: my sax section was already set — it was set for a while. Chuck Wilson and Jack Stooky on altos
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Tate
about_quotes.evidence: The first night that the Alden-Barrett quintet worked, Chuck Wilson had to send in a sub. He had something he couldn’t get out of. And Jerry Dodgion came in...
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Tate interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001
